entries.title: Judges 1:20
entries.author: Adam Clarke (1760–1832)
entries.content: Verse 15 He was afraid of him - He saw that, by his prudent conduct, he was every day gaining increasing influence.

entries.title: Judges 1:25
entries.author: Adam Clarke (1760–1832)
entries.content: Verse 21 That she may be a snare to him - Saul had already determined the condition on which he would give his daughter to David; viz., that he should slay one hundred Philistines: this he supposed he would undertake for the love of Michal, and that he must necessarily perish in the attempt; and thus Michal would become a snare to him.

entries.title: Judges 1:30
entries.author: Adam Clarke (1760–1832)
entries.content: Verse 27 Slew - two hundred men - The Septuagint has only one hundred men. Saul covenanted with David for a hundred; and David himself says, 2Sam 3:14, that he espoused Michal for a hundred: hence it is likely that one hundred is the true reading.

entries.title: Judges 1:35
entries.author: Adam Clarke (1760–1832)
entries.content: Verse 2 Take heed to thyself until the morning - Perhaps the order was given to slay him the next day; and therefore Jonathan charges him to be particularly on his guard at that time, and to hide himself.
